1.14: Firm may not act if the transaction handler or immediate family member is the seller.
1.15: Firm may not act if the transaction handler or immediate family member is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor or licensed conveyancer name and address is required.
3.1.4: Notify Mortgage Completions Team at the 1.11a address if the seller has no legal representation.
3.1.5: Valid old-style driving licence (accompanied by a second document)
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is required.
3.2.4: Notify Mortgage Completions Team at the 1.11a address if the seller has no legal representation.
4.1: A copy of the valuation report is provided if a full valuation was performed.
The mortgage offer will indicate if a full valuation was not obtained.
The solicitor must rely on the Society's report, not the applicant's.
The Society must be contacted if the report is missing.
4.3: Report incorrect valuation assumptions to the 1.11a contact.
4.5b: Send certificate of title to the 1.11a address.
5.1.1: Contact the 1.11a address if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
5.2.1: Contact the 1.11a address if the seller is not the owner or registered proprietor.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are only required if security is adversely affected or the mortgage offer requires them.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted subject to adequate indemnity insurance.
Searches must be monitored by the Property Codes Compliance Board only.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for re-mortgages only.
Requires an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.5.3a: Report breach of conditions to the 1.11a address.
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission required with title deeds.
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents required with title deeds.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development/established use certificates required with title deeds.
5.5.4: Report restrictions affecting marketability to the 1.11a address.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if they affect only part of the security and have satisfactory legal rights of support.
5.7.1c: Report accepted flying freeholds to the 1.11a contact.
5.8.1: Security involving a building converted into up to four flats is accepted if the borrower occupies one flat and the borrower or another flat owner owns the freehold.
5.8.5: Accepted if the mortgage is secured against both the leasehold interest and the freehold interest of the other flat.
5.10.1: Report restrictions on use to the 1.11a address.
5.13.1: Report if borrower is not providing balance of purchase price or proposing a second charge to 1.11a address.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term of 85 years at completion.
Minimum of 55 years remaining at the end of the mortgage term.
5.14.9: Contact the 1.11a address for lease-related matters.
5.14.10: Contact the 1.11a address for service charge matters.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted subject to an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.14.12: Clear ground rent/service charge receipts are not required to be sent.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service must be sent with title deeds.
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords to the 1.11a address.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is not accepted for absent or insolvent landlords.
5.15.2a: Report management company problems to the 1.11a address.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificate must be sent with title deeds.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer form must be sent with title deeds.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association must be sent with title deeds.
5.16.2: Contact the 1.11a address if unable to certify search entry non-relation.
5.17.5a: Power of attorney must be sent with title deeds.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation of power of attorney must be sent with title deeds.
5.19.1: Contact the 1.11a address for affordable housing, shared equity, and shared ownership requirements.
5.20.1: Report solar PV panel leases not meeting UK Finance requirements to Member Services, Emlyn Hughes House, Abbey Road, Barrow in Furness, Cumbria LA14 5PQ.

5.20.4: Disclose Green Deal Plan details to Mortgage Completion Team, Emlyn Hughes House, Abbey Road, Barrow in Furness, Cumbria LA14 5PQ.
6.1.3: Notify 1.11a address if borrower does not take up the mortgage offer.
6.2.1: Report property description discrepancies to the 1.11a address.
6.3.1: Report purchase price issues to the 1.11a address.
6.4.4: For new-builds and newly converted/renovated properties, the Disclosure of Incentives form must be sent prior to completion.
6.4.5: Contact the 1.11a address if the firm will not control all purchase money payments.

6.6.2: Contact the 1.11a address if letting details are missing at completion.
6.6.3: Counterpart or certified copy tenancy agreement required with title deeds.
If the offer makes no reference to tenancies, copies are required prior to completion.
6.6.4: Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) are not accepted.
6.7.1: Accepted schemes: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, Buildzone, Checkmate, Protek, LABC New Home Warranty (individual houses only).
Other schemes require referral to Centralised Mortgage Processing.
6.7.2: Send all warranty certificates and documentation.
6.7.3: Send assignments of building standards indemnity schemes with title deeds.
6.7.4: Property must have a new home warranty scheme to proceed.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate must be sent with title deeds.
6.8.1: Contact the 1.11a address if there is no agreement or bond for an unadopted road or sewer.
6.9.1: Contact the 1.11a address if necessary easements are missing.
6.10.2: Retentions are usually released to the solicitor.
Release directly to borrowers requires written solicitor consent.

6.12.1: Contact the 1.11a address regarding pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options.
6.13.1: Contact the 1.11a address for undischarged improvement or repair grants.
7.3: Consent to mortgage is required from all occupants aged 17 or over.
7.4: Contact the 1.11a address if information accuracy is doubted.
8.1: Solicitor may not advise specified third parties.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policy must be sent with title deeds.
9.2: Indemnity limit must be the higher of the property valuation or purchase price.
10.3: No standard deductions are made from the advance.
10.7: Return advance within one working day via CHAPS.

Reference: Mortgage account number and customer name.

10.11: Interest is charged at the applicable mortgage loan rate if return is delayed.
